title: Weather today: Jammu and Kashmir, Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh on IMD's orange alert; Mercury hits 44.4°C in Jaisalmer - Mint
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
A day after Rajasthan's Jaisalmer reported highest maximum temperature of 44.4°C, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued heat wave alert for 3 May for Coastal Andhra Pradesh. Predicting hailstorm activity over Jammu and Kashmir and thundersquall for Chhattisgarh, the weather office issued orange alert for these regions. While some regions of South peninsular India brace for hot and humid weather conditions today, including Tamil Nadu and Puducherry, Northeastern states of Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura can expect isolated heavy rainfall till 4 May. IMD in its latest weather bulletin forecasted 2-3°C gradual fall in maximum temperatures over Northwest India till 5 May and no significant change thereafter. Western Disturbance is expected to bring downpour in the region in the coming days and hence the change in mercury. “Scattered to fairly widespread light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds (speed reaching 30-50 kmph) likely over Punjab during 03rd-06th; Haryana, Chandigarh during 03rd-06th; isolated to scattered light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds (speed reaching 30-40 kmph) likely over Rajasthan during 03rd-08th; West Uttar Pradesh during 03rd-06th; East Uttar Pradesh during 04th-07th May,” IMD's press release dated 2 May states. IMD warned of fairly widespread to widespread rainfall across Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim and Northeast India this week. Furthermore, isolated to scattered thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds (40-60 kmph) and hailstorm are likely over Western Himalayan region, plains of northwest and adjoining central India until 6 May. IMD added, “Thundersquall (wind speed reaching 50-60 kmph gusting to 70 kmph) likely over West Bengal & Sikkim, Odisha on 04th & 05th; Jharkhand on 04th; Bihar on 04th & 06th May.” Suggesting likelihood of isolated hailstorm activity in Jharkhand and Bihar on 4th May, the Meteorological Department said, “Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim" till 4 May and in Bihar on 4 May. Moving to weather forecast for Central India, IMD predicted isolated light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds for Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh till 7 May and for Maharashtra's Vidarbha region till 6 May. IMD further advised fishermen against venturing into Odisha, West Bengal, Bangladesh coasts and over some parts of northwest and northeast Bay of Bengal on 3 and 4 May. Residents of national capital can expect fall in maximum temperatures by 2-3°C till 5 May amid yellow alert for rains today and tomorrow. IMD predicted, “Partly cloudy sky becoming generally cloudy sky towards afternoon. A spell of very light to light rain accompanied with thunderstorm/lightning and strong winds speed 30-40 kmph gusting to 50 kmph during afternoon to evening.” The minimum temperature will be 1.6°C to 3.0°C above normal at most places and is likely to be in the range of 26°C to 28°C. Meanwhile, the maximum temperature will be -1.6 to -3.0°C below normal and is expected to settle around 36°C to 38°C. title: Weather Today [03 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian

full_text:
Weather Today, 03 May 2026: India is experiencing a sharp shift in weather patterns as heat levels climb across several states while the northern hills show signs of intermittent precipitation. Temperatures in many cities are ranging between Minimum 24°C and Maximum 43°C, pointing toward an early summer surge combined with unstable atmospheric conditions in select regions.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
The Himalayan region, including Kashmir and Uttarakhand, is seeing mixed signals and light rainfall is expected in lower valleys, while higher altitudes may still witness light snowfall. In Srinagar, temperatures remain cool at Minimum 7°C and Maximum 15°C, keeping hill weather stable but changeable.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
Rapid heating is driven by delayed pre-monsoon activity and dry western winds. Central and northwestern India are particularly affected, with cities like Ahmedabad reaching Minimum 28°C and Maximum 43°C. Reduced cloud cover and rising solar radiation are accelerating daytime heating across plains.

- Uttar Pradesh: Persistent warm conditions up to 40°C
- Telangana & Andhra Regions: High humidity heat stress zones

How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?
Heat stress is increasing across urban India, affecting travel, health and electricity demand. Peak afternoon temperatures above 40°C in multiple cities are likely to increase dehydration cases and energy consumption by nearly 15–20% in major metro regions.

FAQ’s: All About the Weather Today
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Yes, heat alerts in Rajasthan, Gujarat and Delhi-NCR suggest avoiding midday travel.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes, most northern and western states will see temperatures above 40°C.

Light and scattered rain is possible in Delhi-NCR and parts of South India.
4. Where will it snow today?
Only light snowfall is possible in higher Himalayan regions.

Yes, temperatures are rising earlier than seasonal averages in several states.

title: Weather Today: Yellow alert issued for Delhi-NCR, check IMD forecast for Kerala, West Bengal, J&K and othe - The Economic Times
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast that rainfall in May is expected to be above normal across most parts of the country, except for some areas in east, northeast, and east-central India where below-normal rainfall is likely.
Delhi Weather Today
Rain and thunderstorms lashed parts of Delhi NCR on Saturday night, providing much-needed respite from the scorching heat that had gripped the region over the past few days. Dark clouds rapidly built up over the national capital and surrounding areas, with rainfall reported in several locations, including the Delhi–Noida border. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow alert for the next two days, cautioning residents about possible rain and thunderstorms.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the maximum temperature today is likely to hover between 36°C and 38°C, while the minimum is expected to range from 26°C to 28°C. The yellow alert issued for the next two days points to the possibility of
weather disturbances. Meanwhile, the city’s air quality remained in the moderate category.
Kerala Weather Today
The weather department has issued a yellow alert for districts in Kerala, cautioning about heavy rainfall, thunderstorms, and strong winds over the coming days. Districts including Kollam, Pathanamthitta, and Alappuzha are expected to receive heavy showers.
Jammu and Kashmir Weather Today
Jammu and Kashmir will continue to receive rain/snow with thunderstorms.
In Northeast India, widespread rainfall continues across all states, including Assam and Meghalaya, where heavy showers are expected to persist.
In South India, rainfall activity is set to continue across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Karnataka. Heavy showers are expected particularly over Tamil Nadu and Kerala, along with thunderstorms and gusty winds.
In East India, West Bengal and Sikkim are likely to experience continued widespread rainfall, along with thunderstorms and strong winds. Meanwhile, Bihar, Odisha, and Jharkhand are expected to receive light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms.
